2:I[2972,["972","static/chunks/972-252bc37210ca6a4d.js","227","static/chunks/app/tasks/%5Bid%5D/page-78d550bc47053b99.js"],""]

6:I[6423,[],""]
7:I[5306,["972","static/chunks/972-252bc37210ca6a4d.js","302","static/chunks/302-9f6a90162c2d1157.js","185","static/chunks/app/layout-1d4e32c367db4b03.js"],"SiteHeader"]
3:T4f4,Produce a Comparison report on three starter investing approaches under a $10,000 first-year portfolio: (P1) Passive index ETFs (S&P 500 / Total Market / Bond aggregate), (P2) Target-date / robo-advisor managed (Betterment / Wealthfront / Vanguard target-date fund), (P3) Active stock-picking (DIY through Fidelity / Schwab / E*TRADE), across 5 dimensions: (D1) expected return / volatility, (D2) annual cost incl. expense ratio + advisory fee, (D3) tax efficiency, (D4) time required per month, (D5) behavioural risk (panic-sell during crash). Ground in >= 120 sandbox URLs and cite >= 60. (A) `__SHOPPING__` >= 12 product pages on personal finance books / calculators / planners + price + rating. (B) `__REDDIT__` >= 30 threads from /f/personalfinance, /f/investing, /f/Bogleheads, /f/financialindependence, /f/wallstreetbets, /f/stocks. (C) `__WIKIPEDIA__` >= 25 articles, mandatory: Index fund, Exchange-traded fund, Modern portfolio theory, Sharpe ratio, Expense ratio, Tax-loss harvesting, Robo-advisor, Behavioral finance, Dollar cost averaging, S&P 500. Output a 3 x 5 matrix rated {best/acceptable/poor} with cited evidence per cell. End with 'who should pick which path' (3 user persona paragraphs). NO TOP-10. Format: markdown links only. Sandbox URLs only.5:["id","dr_cross_deep_0013","d"]
